preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that places a premium on approach play and total strokes gained. The course rewards players who hit greens from distance and avoid big numbers rather than pure distance off the tee. This event matters as a mid-summer stop where form and course fit can separate contenders from the field.
preview.modelExplainer: SG Approach (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) carry the heaviest weight, followed by SG Putting (16.8%) and SG Around the Green (13.3%). Driving accuracy and bogey avoidance each sit at 3.9% and 3.0%, underscoring the importance of precision and clean scoring over raw power. The model tilts toward players who excel on approach and maintain steady ball-striking.
preview.pickApproach: We tier bets by combining tournament rank and power rank to identify value. Top-tier names like Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler lead our outright and top-10 lists. Mid-tier plays focus on players with strong SG Approach or SG Total marks who sit outside the top 20 in power rank, offering price upside.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Model ranks Jake Knapp first with 2.002 SG total while Scheffler sits second at 1.868 SG total entering the 144-player field.
article.introduction: The tournament model places heavy emphasis on SG APP (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) at TPC Twin Cities. Jake Knapp leads the rankings with 2.002 SG total and 1.285 SG putting, ahead of Scottie Scheffler at 1.868 SG total. No outright, top-10, or top-20 targets were generated this week.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: The 3M Open at TPC Twin Cities features a 144-player field with course weights favoring SG APP at 25.8% and SG Total at 22.8%. Jake Knapp ranks first in the model with 2.002 SG total while Scottie Scheffler ranks second at 1.868 SG total.

sections.heading: Course Factors
sections.body: SG APP carries the highest weight at 25.8%, followed by SG Total at 22.8% and SG PUT at 16.8%. Austin Smotherman ranks seventh with 0.874 SG APP, while Hideki Matsuyama ranks fourth with 0.635 SG APP.

sections.heading: Weekly Betting Angles
sections.body: Sudarshan Yellamaraju ranks third with 1.34 SG total and 0.474 SG APP. David Lipsky ranks fifth with 0.89 SG total and 0.524 SG ATG. Maverick McNealy ranks sixth with 0.994 SG total and 0.31 SG OTT.

sections.heading: Post-Open Workload and Motivation
sections.body: Jake Knapp enters with 4 rounds over two weeks and FedExCup rank #39 after missing the cut at The Open. Scottie Scheffler played 6 rounds over two weeks with FedExCup rank #1 after a top-5 Open finish. Kurt Kitayama logged 8 rounds over two weeks with FedExCup rank #32.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: No outright targets were generated this week.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: No top-10 targets were generated this week.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: No top-20 targets were generated this week.

sections.heading: Players to Approach Cautiously
sections.body: Rasmus Højgaard ranks tenth with 0.823 SG total but logged 6 rounds over two weeks and sits at FedExCup rank #123. Jordan Smith ranks seventeenth with 0.465 SG total after 8 rounds over two weeks and FedExCup rank #66.

article.conclusion: Model data shows no outright, top-10, or top-20 targets this week. Jake Knapp leads with 2.002 SG total and Scottie Scheffler follows at 1.868 SG total as the primary model-supported names.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.
